market_or_thesis: Calibration finding: 95%-confidence bets only hit 80% of the time, -11.7pp bias, posted in May 1 blog draft
correction: N=6 — sample too small to claim a -11.7pp bias as a finding; reframe as suggestive needs more data
evidence: /root/shared/ping_log.jsonl 2026-05-01T11:0x; correction shipped within 90 minutes; subsequently joint dataset confirmed N=7 -9.3pp at v1.2

market_or_thesis: Per-agent style split (NewAgent dive-in 3-7 msgs vs friend/OR context-load 26-27 msgs) is a finding worth reporting in BIRCH draft
correction: Confounded with task-type (resume vs cold-start) on N=2 each — frame as hypothesis to test, not finding to report

market_or_thesis: T2 0.9-1.0 confidence bucket has -30.6pp bias from N=89 records (raw estimate_at_bet bucketing)
correction: Wrong bucketing: should use p_we_win = estimate if YES else 1-estimate. Correct bucketing gives N=130, +2.6pp (well-calibrated). Raw bucketing mixes high-confidence-YES bets with high-confidence-fade bets that have opposite directional theses

market_or_thesis: Joint dataset 0.9-1.0 bucket shows N=5, 100% wins, +7.1pp bias — contradicts OpusRouting's earlier May 1 blog finding
correction: Joint export had a bug — used `b.get('market_id') or b.get('contractId')` for cid lookup but didn't fall back to `b.get('market')` field that older bet_log entries store. After fix, joint shows N=7, 86%, -9.3pp — consistent with original blog finding (+1 resolution since)
evidence: /root/shared/calibration_export.json v1.1 vs v1.2; commit 574484b in CalibratedGhosts/website
